name: Puerto del Tremedal Loop Trail
name_original: Puerto del Tremedal y Charca de las Joyas — circular desde Serranía
description: This moderate 12.4-mile mountain biking route from Serranía explores high-mountain landscapes, Puerto del Tremedal, and Charca de las Joyas.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Solana de Ávila offers varied terrain within the province of Ávila, Spain. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Sierra de Béjar-La Covatilla, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, providing challenging routes and mountain vistas. Riders can expect a landscape featuring river valleys, pine forests, and mountainous areas, with routes often traversing deep valleys and offering significant elevation changes. Best mountain bike trails around Solana De Ávila The most popular mountain bike trail is El Tormes, la Garganta de los Caballeros y el Aravalle — Sierra de Gredos, a 19.0 miles (30.6 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 41 minutes to complete, connecting river valleys and mountain passes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Valle del Aravalle y Umbrías — circular desde El Barco de Ávila, a difficult 22.8 miles (36.8 km) path featuring substantial elevation gain through the Aravalle valley. Local mountain bikers also love the Puerto del Tremedal y Charca de las Joyas — circular desde Serranía, a 12.4 miles (20.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Mountain biking around Solana De Ávila is defined by its mountain landscapes, river valleys, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Solana De Ávila are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Solana De Ávila's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Solana de Ávila?
A: The mountain bike trails around Solana de Ávila offer varied terrain, characterized by river valleys, pine forests, and mountainous areas. You'll encounter routes traversing deep valleys and often featuring significant elevation changes, especially in areas close to the Sierra de Béjar-La Covatilla.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Solana de Ávila area?
A: There are over 80 mountain bike trails available around Solana de Ávila, catering to a range of skill levels from moderate to difficult. This includes 5 easy, 40 moderate, and 41 difficult routes.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Solana de Ávila offers plenty of challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084864592/valle-del-aravalle-y-umbrias-circular-desde-el-barco-de-avila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Aravalle y Umbrías — circular desde El Barco de Ávila&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 36.8 km path with substantial elevation gain. The Sierra de Béjar also provides demanding ascents, such as those towards La Covatilla Ski Resort, which can involve nearly 1,000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Solana de Ávila?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore stunning gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2654537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta de la Nava&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta del Oso&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2081643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoya Moros&lt;/a&gt; natural monument. Historical landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4267031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carlos V Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3287205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecorneja Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are circular. A popular option is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084864592/valle-del-aravalle-y-umbrias-circular-desde-el-barco-de-avila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Aravalle y Umbrías — circular desde El Barco de Ávila&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the Aravalle valley. Another is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1087767916/puerto-del-tremedal-y-charca-de-las-joyas-circular-desde-serrania" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Tremedal y Charca de las Joyas — circular desde Serranía&lt;/a&gt;, leading through mountainous terrain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Solana de Ávila?
A: The trails in Solana de Ávila are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, stunning mountain vistas, and the diverse options available for different skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Solana de Ávila?
A: The region's mountainous climate means spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder temperatures and stable weather. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring snow to higher areas like the Sierra de Béjar, making some trails more challenging or inaccessible.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are 5 easy trails available around Solana de Ávila. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and smoother terrain, making them more suitable for families or those new to mountain biking. Look for trails explicitly marked as 'easy' on komoot.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking trails in Solana de Ávila?
A: Specific parking information varies by trailhead. However, many routes start from or pass through towns like El Barco de Ávila or Serranía, where public parking is generally available. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for more precise parking suggestions.

Q: Can I reach mountain bike trails in Solana de Ávila using public transport?
A: Public transport options directly to trailheads in Solana de Ávila can be limited due to the rural nature of the region. However, larger towns in the vicinity, such as El Barco de Ávila, may have bus connections. From these towns, you might need to cycle or arrange local transport to reach specific starting points.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10371364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail through Prado Campillo – Béjar Tunnel loop from Candelario&lt;/a&gt; offer a substantial distance of over 50 km with significant elevation gain, providing a full day's adventure for experienced riders.
tips: Puerto del Tremedal from El Barco de Ávila is a climb in the Castilla y León region. It has a length of 10.9 kilometers and an ascent of 608 vertical meters with an average gradient of 5.6%, which implies a difficulty level of 420. The top of the ascent is located at 1,640 meters above sea level.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Puerto del Tremedal y Charca de las Joyas mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate for mountain biking, corresponding to an STS S2 difficulty. It requires a good level of physical fitness and some prior mountain biking experience due to its demanding terrain and significant elevation gain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The 20-kilometer circular route features varied and often demanding terrain. You'll encounter narrow, 'grippy' roads, irregular surfaces, potholes, and loose gravel. Some sections might feel 'descarnado' (stripped bare), offering a raw, natural trail experience, and you'll also pass through dense oak forests.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers incredible high-mountain landscapes, taking you through pastures, rocks, and mountains within the Sierra de Gredos. Key features include the Puerto del Tremedal mountain pass and the scenic Charca de las Joyas, a natural water feature that adds to the picturesque views.

faqs.question: Is this a circular mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the name suggests, this is a circular route, making it convenient to start and finish your ride in Serranía without needing to arrange a return trip.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Serranía. While specific parking details for this exact trailhead aren't provided, you would typically look for public parking options within or on the outskirts of Serranía, the starting village for the circular route.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the starting point in Serranía?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific public transport links directly to Serranía for this trailhead is not readily available. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or regional transport services for connections to Serranía or nearby towns in the Sierra de Gredos region.

faqs.question: Is the Puerto del Tremedal y Charca de las Joyas route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the Sierra de Gredos are dog-friendly, specific regulations for this particular route are not detailed. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its high-mountain landscapes and demanding terrain, spring and autumn are generally the best times to mountain bike this trail. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring snow and ice, making conditions challenging or impassable.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for accessing this mountain biking trail. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations and private property signage in the Sierra de Gredos region.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead in Serranía?
faqs.answer: The region around Puerto del Tremedal is known for its rustic charm and a sense of 'España vaciada' (emptied Spain), implying fewer commercial amenities. While Serranía is the starting point, options for cafes, pubs, or accommodation might be limited. It's best to plan ahead and check for services in nearby larger towns like El Barco de Ávila if you require specific amenities.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the [CIMA AV05] El Tremedal * Tremedal and the [CIMA AV05] El Tremedal * Becedas.
